title: Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee March 18-19, 2025

source.name: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

summary: FOMC minutes from the March 18-19, 2025 meeting reveal policymakers continued reviewing their monetary policy framework with focus on maximum employment indicators, and participants indicated support for reconsidering the 'shortfalls language' in their statement. Financial markets showed notable shifts over the intermeeting period with Treasury yields declining ~25 bps, equity prices falling (especially technology and cyclical sectors), credit spreads widening, and the dollar depreciating, reflecting increased perceived risks rather than changed baseline expectations.
key_points: FOMC participants generally supported the current description of maximum employment as not directly measurable and changing over time, but indicated it would be appropriate to reconsider the 'shortfalls language' in the statement
Core labor market indicators discussed include unemployment rate, job vacancies, employment-to-population ratio, and labor force participation rate
Treasury yields declined ~25 bps over the intermeeting period, led by decreases in real yields reflecting lower expected real rates and lower real term premiums
Market-based measures suggested inflation expectations remained well anchored despite near-term measures rising amid trade-related developments
The implied average federal funds rate path from futures shifted noticeably lower for horizons beyond mid-year, but the modal path from options was little changed
Survey respondents attached noticeably higher probabilities to both lower GDP growth outcomes and higher inflation outcomes for 2025 compared to January
ON RRP facility reached a post-2021 low of under $59 billion in mid-February

Repo market conditions tightened with increased sensitivity to Treasury bill issuance; manager interpreted this as normalization rather than concern but noted it could be precursor to tighter reserve conditions
why_it_matters: These minutes provide insight into the Fed's ongoing framework review and how participants view employment goals, which could influence future policy communications. The market dynamics described—equity selloff concentrated in technology/cyclicals, rising volatility, and currency weakness—signal investor concern about U.S. economic outperformance being challenged by trade policy uncertainty and weaker data.
macro_context: The minutes describe a market environment where investors priced in elevated tail risks to the U.S. outlook, with elevated uncertainty around both growth (lower) and inflation (higher) outcomes for 2025. This represents a material shift in risk perception relative to baseline expectations, though the baseline itself appeared little changed according to options and survey modal paths.
market_structure_context: The manager noted repo market conditions continued tightening with increased sensitivity to Treasury bill issuance, though interpreted this as normalization. ON RRP usage declined to post-2021 lows under $59 billion. These dynamics warrant monitoring as they could be precursors to tighter reserve conditions.
important_numbers: ~25 bps decline in nominal Treasury yields
under $59 billion ON RRP low in mid-February
2025 GDP growth: higher probability of lower outcomes
2025 inflation: higher probability of higher outcomes

human_review.notes: Historical record (13 months old). Source is primary Federal Reserve document with high reliability. Document discusses both macro catalysts (growth/inflation risks, policy uncertainty) and market structure elements (repo market, ON RRP). Appropriate for both topics in scope.

notes: Source is FOMC minutes - a primary official document from the Federal Reserve. Document truncated in source body but key content from beginning sections is captured. Historical record from March 2025 (13 months prior to assessment date); lower confidence accordingly but content remains authoritative for historical research. Discusses both macro catalysts (market risk repricing, growth/inflation uncertainty) and market structure (repo market operations, ON RRP facility levels).
